title: Calcium result review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports clinician-reviewed vitamin D, urine calcium, renal function, DXA and renal-tract imaging in a confirmed or probable primary-hyperparathyroidism context.
examination.rationale: The exact secondary-assessment section supports measured vital signs, focused endocrine assessment and previous specialist correspondence only when performed or available, without ordering tests.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed vitamin D, urine calcium, renal function, DXA and renal-tract imaging in a confirmed or probable primary-hyperparathyroidism context.

source.title: Hyperparathyroidism (primary): diagnosis, assessment and initial management

investigations.text: Supports thirst, polyuria, constipation, osteoporosis, fragility-fracture and renal-stone context; repeat albumin-adjusted calcium, concurrent PTH and specialist-advice documentation when clinician-selected.
investigations.rationale: The exact NICE calcium and PTH section supports associated symptom, relevant-negative, patient-concern and clinician-reviewed endocrine laboratory documentation for a possible primary-hyperparathyroidism context; it does not cover every cause of abnormal calcium.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports thirst, polyuria, constipation, osteoporosis, fragility-fracture and renal-stone context; repeat albumin-adjusted calcium, concurrent PTH and specialist-advice documentation when clinician-selected.

source.title: Hyperparathyroidism (primary): diagnosis, assessment and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.10 — albumin-adjusted calcium and PTH testing

referral.text: Supports clinician-arranged calcium, PTH, renal-function, DXA and specialist monitoring with recommendation-specific qualifiers; no interval is auto-generated.
referral.rationale: The exact monitoring section supports clinician concern, entered plan, completed safety-net discussion and arranged follow-up only when documented; no interval or treatment is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged calcium, PTH, renal-function, DXA and specialist monitoring with recommendation-specific qualifiers; no interval is auto-generated.

source.title: Hyperparathyroidism (primary): diagnosis, assessment and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.4.10–1.6.2 and monitoring table — follow-up and monitoring

source_limitations: Albumin, corrected or ionised calcium type, exact value, repeat status, PTH, renal function, vitamin D, symptoms, cause, diagnosis, urgency, treatment and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
